REIS IA EFFECTS DESIGN SYSTEM v1.0

Effects Library

Four signature effects create the Reis IA visual identity. Each is a unique synthesis, not a direct copy from any reference. Use them with restraint — the effects are powerful precisely because they are rare.

[01] Sapphire Scanner

A rotating conic gradient border where a blue light segment slowly orbits the card perimeter. The narrow beam (~15% of the circle) creates a scanning effect that evokes systematic precision and analytical methodology.

Speed: 8s default (configurable) Usage: 1-2 featured cards per page max Easing: linear infinite
Speed: 8s

Sapphire Scanner

A blue light segment sweeps the card perimeter at a configurable speed. The narrow beam (~15% of the circle) creates a scanning effect.

Standard Card

For comparison: a standard card with a static border. The rotating border is reserved for 1-2 featured cards per page.
Sapphire Scanner CSS
@property --border-angle {
  syntax: "<angle>";
  initial-value: 0deg;
  inherits: false;
}

.card-rotating-border {
  background:
    linear-gradient(135deg, var(--surface-2), var(--surface-3)) padding-box,
    conic-gradient(
      from var(--border-angle),
      rgba(255,255,255,0.08) 0%,
      rgba(255,255,255,0.08) 75%,
      rgba(74,144,255,0.5) 85%,
      rgba(74,144,255,0.8) 90%,
      rgba(74,144,255,0.5) 95%,
      rgba(255,255,255,0.08) 100%
    ) border-box;
  border: 1px solid transparent;
  border-radius: 12px;
  animation: rotate-border 8s linear infinite;
}

@keyframes rotate-border {
  to { --border-angle: 360deg; }
}
[02] Cool Ambient Pools

Subtle radial gradient light pools positioned at section corners. Blue-tinted pools appear on key sections (hero, CTA areas), while neutral white-alpha pools appear on secondary sections. Corner positions alternate to create visual rhythm.

Blue pool: rgba(74, 144, 255, 0.06) Neutral pool: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.03) Pattern: Alternate corners per section
Section on Surface-0 -- Blue pool at top-left

Blue-tinted ambient pools appear on key sections (hero, CTA areas). The radial gradient uses rgba(74, 144, 255, 0.06) for a subtle glow.

Section on Surface-1 -- Neutral pool at bottom-right

Secondary sections use neutral (white-alpha) pools. Corner positions alternate to create visual rhythm across the page.
Ambient Pool CSS
/* Blue ambient pool (hero/CTA sections) */
.section-hero::before {
  content: '';
  position: absolute;
  top: -20%;
  left: -10%;
  width: 60%;
  height: 140%;
  background: radial-gradient(
    ellipse at center,
    rgba(74, 144, 255, 0.06) 0%,
    transparent 70%
  );
  pointer-events: none;
}

/* Neutral pool (secondary sections) */
.section-alt::after {
  content: '';
  position: absolute;
  bottom: -20%;
  right: -10%;
  width: 50%;
  height: 140%;
  background: radial-gradient(
    ellipse at center,
    rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.03) 0%,
    transparent 70%
  );
  pointer-events: none;
}
Placement Guide
Section 1 (Hero) Blue ambient, centered on headline
Section 2 Neutral pool, bottom-left corner
Section 3 Neutral pool, top-right corner
Section 4 (CTA) Blue ambient, centered on CTA
Section 5 Neutral pool, bottom-right corner
Pattern Continue alternating...
[03] H1-B Hourglass Watermarks

Large hourglass silhouettes (200-300px) placed as background watermarks at 3-5% opacity behind key sections. The hourglass is the sole brand mark — it appears in time/efficiency/urgency sections and wherever a visual anchor is needed.

Size: 200-300px Opacity: 3-5% (0.03-0.05) Position: absolute, pointer-events: none
Top-Right — 3%

Content sits naturally on top of the watermark. The low opacity ensures the mark is felt but never competes with readability.

Center — 4%

Content sits naturally on top of the watermark. The low opacity ensures the mark is felt but never competes with readability.

Bottom-Left — 5%

Content sits naturally on top of the watermark. The low opacity ensures the mark is felt but never competes with readability.

Watermark CSS
.section-watermark {
  position: relative;
  overflow: hidden;
}

.section-watermark .watermark {
  position: absolute;
  top: -20px;
  right: -20px;
  width: 240px;
  height: 336px;
  opacity: 0.04;
  pointer-events: none;
  z-index: 0;
}

/* Hide on mobile to reduce visual noise */
@media (max-width: 767px) {
  .section-watermark .watermark {
    display: none;
  }
}
[04] Surface Depth Harmony

The 5-tier surface system combined with white-alpha borders and cool ambient pools creates physical depth perception without shadows. This is the foundational visual technique — all other effects layer on top of this structure.

S-0 Void
Page background #000000
S-1 Base
Alt sections #0A0A0A
S-2 Raised
Cards #111111
S-3 Elevated
Hover/inputs #161616
S-4 Float
Dropdowns #1A1A1A

Each tier narrows visually, representing proximity to the viewer. Closer = lighter = smaller footprint.

Surface Tiers

5 background values from pure black to #1A1A1A. Depth through lightness, not shadows.

White-Alpha Borders

5-20% opacity borders that harmonize with any surface tier. Higher tiers get more visible borders.

Ambient Pools

Cool blue radial gradients add directional depth to the surface stack. Applied strategically, not globally.

[05] Grain / Noise Texture

A subtle SVG-generated noise texture overlaid at 2-4% opacity on sections to add analog dimension to flat dark backgrounds. The effect prevents the "digital void" feel without being consciously visible.

Opacity: 2-4% (default 3%) Technique: SVG feTurbulence (no external file) z-index: 1 (above bg, below content)
Opacity:
Surface-0 with grain

The noise overlay adds analog texture to flat dark backgrounds, preventing the "digital void" feel.

Surface-0 without grain

Pure flat black. Compare with the left side to see the subtle texture difference at the current opacity level.
Grain Overlay CSS
.grain::after {
  content: "";
  position: absolute;
  inset: 0;
  pointer-events: none;
  opacity: 0.03;
  background-image: url("data:image/svg+xml,...");
  z-index: 1;
}
[06] Glassmorphism

Blur and saturate backdrop-filter effects for navigation, glass cards, and overlays. The saturate(180%) boost prevents blur from washing out underlying content.

Navigation (scrolled)
blur(20px) saturate(180%)
Glass Card
blur(16px) saturate(180%)
Mobile Menu Overlay
blur(24px) none
Stats Overlay
blur(12px) none
[07] Usage Rules
Do
  • Use Sapphire Scanner on 1-2 featured cards per page max
  • Alternate ambient pool positions between sections
  • Keep grain opacity at 2-4% — subtle, not visible
  • Hide watermark motifs on mobile screens
  • Limit blue glow elements to max 2 per viewport
Don't
  • Apply rotating border to every card — dilutes the effect
  • Stack multiple ambient pools in the same corner
  • Use grain above 5% opacity — it becomes distracting
  • Place watermarks where they interfere with text readability
  • Combine glassmorphism with grain on the same element
REIS IA

Design System v1.0 — the single source of truth for the Reis IA brand identity, tokens, components, and patterns.
